mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound with no upward drift signals continued accommodative policy, lowering the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and supporting a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX exhibits negative mean return during sustained low-rate periods with p<0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: In triggered episodes, SPX forward 20d returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the effect size is not statistically distinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates broad-based consumer and residential investment strength that compounds over a quarter, reinforcing durable demand momentum and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days; neither series alone captures this cross-sector reinforcement.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 5%
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized consumer and housing strength
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes fail to show statistically higher 60d forward returns than either single-series trigger alone (interaction term insignificant at p>0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, decelerating industrial production growth signals approaching capacity constraints and margin compression, causing S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter to underperform relative to early/mid-cycle periods with the same INDPRO growth rate.
failure_condition: INDPRO deceleration in late-cycle windows is not followed by below-median 60d forward returns relative to full-sample baseline, or the regime split shows no significant difference (p>0.10) in mean forward return versus early/mid-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) reflects strengthening labor demand that is quickly priced by equity markets, producing a short-lived positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following PAYEMS acceleration show Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean return is not significantly different from unconditional 5-day mean (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year rate crosses above 3%, markets anticipate accelerated monetary tightening, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20d returns are positive in more than 55% of instances following the CPI threshold crossing, or the mean return difference versus non-triggered periods is not significant at p<0.05.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing-recovery regime characterized by housing starts (HOUST) rising off cyclical lows, sustained increases in HOUST forecast broad demand recovery and support positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter; this relationship is muted or reversed once housing starts are already at elevated cycle levels.
failure_condition: The positive HOUST-return relationship fails to hold with Rank IC < 0.03 during recovery-regime subsamples, or the direction flips to negative with p<0.05 significance in recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) combined with an unexpected federal funds rate increase (FEDFUNDS) creates a 'good news is bad news' dynamic where markets fear accelerated tightening despite labor strength, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week; the effect requires both signals jointly and is absent when either occurs alone.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM change > 200k AND FEDFUNDS increases in same month
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes show non-negative mean 5d forward returns, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from the sum of individual PAYEMS and FEDFUNDS effects (p>0.10).
